OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT):

response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion: As someone who has lived through the evolution of social media, I must say that I'm quite perplexed by the proliferation of fake content on these platforms. In my day, we didn't need social media to spread misinformation - we had newspapers and word of mouth for that. But now, with the likes of Facebook, Twitter, and Instagram, it seems like fake news is everywhere. The problem is, it's not just about the content itself, but about how it spreads so quickly and easily. I've seen friends share articles and videos without even reading the fine print, and then they're outraged when they realize they've been duped. It's like they're craving a good scandal or a juicy conspiracy theory. I've tried to explain to them that just because it's on social media doesn't mean it's true, but they just brush it off. It's like they're addicted to the drama and the excitement, even if it's fake. As someone with a high openness score, I'm naturally curious and want to learn about the world, but I also know how important it is to verify information before sharing it. I wish more people would take the time to fact-check and think critically about what they're reading and watching. It's not that hard, folks. Just take a deep breath, slow down, and ask yourself, 'Is this really true?' Ah, the joys of social media. I'll stick to my books and birdwatching, thank you very much. My friends say I'm just a relic of the past, but I say, why fix what ain't broke?
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: I have lived through the evolution of social media and have seen how it has changed the way people consume and share information. I have high openness and agreeableness scores, which makes me naturally curious and wanting to learn about the world. I have also been a victim of fake news in the past, which has made me cautious and critical of the information I consume. I wish more people would take the time to fact-check and think critically about what they're reading and watching. I believe that social media platforms have a responsibility to regulate the spread of fake news and provide users with accurate and trustworthy information. I will continue to avoid social media and stick to traditional sources of information, such as books and newspapers. I will also continue to educate my friends and family on the importance of critical thinking and fact-checking. I will not engage with people who spread misinformation or conspiracy theories, and I will instead try to have an open and respectful conversation with them. I will also continue to use my hobbies, such as reading and birdwatching, to stay informed and engaged with the world around me.
